issues.title: Zero analytics instrumentation in all practitioner handlers — practitioner activation, retention, and feature adoption are unmeasurable
issues.fix: Wire trackEvent() and trackFunnel() calls into all three practitioner handler files. The analytics library (workers/src/lib/analytics.js) already defines EVENTS.CLIENT_ADD, EVENTS.CLIENT_REMOVE, EVENTS.CLUSTER_CREATE, EVENTS.CLUSTER_SYNTHESIZE and FUNNELS.PRACTITIONER with steps: register, first_client, first_synthesis, upgrade_guide. Import { trackEvent, trackFunnel, EVENTS, FUNNELS, captureRequestContext } from '../lib/analytics.js' in each handler. In practitioner.js: (1) POST /api/practitioner/register handler — after successful registration INSERT, add ctx.waitUntil(trackFunnel(env, userId, 'practitioner', 'register')); (2) POST /api/practitioner/clients/add — after successful INSERT into practitioner_clients, add ctx.waitUntil(trackEvent(env, EVENTS.CLIENT_ADD, { userId, properties: { clientEmail: '[redacted]', method: 'direct_add' }, requestContext: captureRequestContext(request) })); also check if this is the first client and call trackFunnel(env, userId, 'practitioner', 'first_client'); (3) POST /api/practitioner/clients/invite — after successful invite INSERT, add trackEvent(env, 'client_invite', { userId, properties: { method: 'email_invite' }, requestContext: captureRequestContext(request) }); (4) DELETE /api/practitioner/clients/:id — after successful DELETE, add trackEvent(env, EVENTS.CLIENT_REMOVE, { userId, requestContext: captureRequestContext(request) }); (5) POST /api/practitioner/clients/:id/session-brief — after successful LLM call, add trackEvent(env, 'session_brief_generate', { userId, requestContext: captureRequestContext(request) }) and trackFunnel(env, userId, 'practitioner', 'first_synthesis'). In session-notes.js: (1) POST notes — after successful INSERT, add trackEvent(env, 'session_note_create', { userId, properties: { clientId, shareWithAi: Boolean(share_with_ai) }, requestContext: captureRequestContext(request) }); (2) PUT notes/:noteId — after successful UPDATE, add trackEvent(env, 'session_note_update', { userId, requestContext: captureRequestContext(request) }); (3) PUT ai-context — after successful upsert, add trackEvent(env, 'ai_context_update', { userId, properties: { clientId }, requestContext: captureRequestContext(request) }). In practitioner-directory.js: PUT /api/practitioner/directory-profile — after successful upsert, add trackEvent(env, 'directory_profile_update', { userId, properties: { isPublic: Boolean(visible) }, requestContext: captureRequestContext(request) }). All trackEvent calls MUST use ctx.waitUntil() pattern or be fire-and-forget with .catch() — analytics must never block or crash user requests. Use captureRequestContext(request) instead of passing request directly when inside waitUntil. Verify by searching for 'trackEvent' in all three files after implementation — each file should have at least 2 calls.

issues.title: No weekly practitioner digest email — practitioners have no recurring engagement loop pulling them back to the platform between sessions
issues.fix: Add a weekly digest cron job that emails practitioners a summary of their workspace activity. Implementation: (1) In workers/src/handlers/cron.js, add a new function sendPractitionerWeeklyDigests(env, log) that runs on a weekly schedule (check day of week === 1 for Monday). Query: SELECT p.id, p.user_id, u.email, u.display_name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM practitioner_clients pc WHERE pc.practitioner_id = p.id) as client_count, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM session_notes sn WHERE sn.practitioner_id = p.id AND sn.created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days') as notes_this_week, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM practitioner_clients pc2 JOIN charts c ON c.user_id = pc2.client_user_id WHERE pc2.practitioner_id = p.id AND c.created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days') as new_charts_this_week FROM practitioners p JOIN users u ON p.user_id = u.id WHERE u.tier IN ('practitioner', 'agency', 'guide', 'white_label'). (2) For each practitioner with at least 1 client, call sendPractitionerWeeklyDigest(env, { email, displayName, clientCount, notesThisWeek, newChartsThisWeek }). (3) In workers/src/lib/email.js, add sendPractitionerWeeklyDigest template: subject 'Your Prime Self Weekly Summary', body with stats grid showing client count, notes written this week, new client charts, and a CTA to open the practitioner dashboard. (4) Wire into the cron handler's scheduled() function — add to the existing cron dispatch. (5) Respect notification preferences from CMO-012 — add weeklyDigest: true default to the notification_preferences JSONB.
notes: Depends on CMO-012 for notification_preferences column in practitioners table
The cron system already runs nightly — see workers/src/handlers/cron.js for the existing pattern
Keep the SQL efficient — this runs for ALL practitioner-tier users, so use a single query with subqueries, not N+1
Use Resend batch API if available, otherwise sequential sends with error isolation
